e-Edition

TRENDING:

Matt Ludwig is one good day away from advancing to the 2024 Summer Olympics in France.

A graduate of Lake Catholic, Ludwig was one of 12 pole vaulters who advanced in the preliminary round of competition on June 21 at the Olympic Trials in Eugene, Ore. The competition resumes on Sunday, June 23, with the top three finishers advancing to the Olympics representing the United States starting July 26.

Ludwig, 27, cleared all four of his heights on the first try on Jun 21, clearing 17-8 1/2, 18-0 1/2, 18-4 1/2 and 18-6 1/2. He, along with Sam Kendricks, passed at the 18-8 1/4 height as others in the competition struggled and most of them were eliminated at that height.

Heading into the finals on June 23, Kendricks and Ludwig are tied for sixth. That’s because five other vaulters who did not pass at the 18-8 1/4 height cleared the mark, Austin Miller on his first try and the group of Chris Nilson, Keaton Daniel, Jacob Wooten and Zachery Bradford all clearing it after a miss.

Ludwig is aiming for a return trip the the Olympics after participating in the 2020 games in Tokyo. Ironically, the man Ludwig is tied with — Kendricks — is partly responsible for the berth in Tokyo, as Kendricks tested positive for COVID-19, which allowed Ludwig to move up from his alternate role to the Olympic roster.
